summary: Solid soundboard capture of a unique 1985 show – crisp on the good moments, a bit rough in spots, with standout performances of Scarlet Begonias and She Belongs to Me.
review: The recording is generally praised as one of the best sources for this concert, offering clear, energetic sound though a few reviewers note occasional muddiness that could benefit from EQ. Musically the show shines with memorable renditions of Scarlet Begonias, She Belongs to Me, Comes A Time and Brokedown Palace, and an unusually structured second set that many fans find unforgettable.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and highlight strong playing and a unique setlist; a few note recording roughness and one gives a 0‑star rating, pulling the average down to around 4.1–4.3.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp and clear on most tracks; some reviewers mention tin‑canny or rough spots that could use slight EQ, but overall considered one of the best audio sources for this show.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The second set is noted for its unusual ordering – pre‑drums sequence of Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain > Samson And Delilah > She Belongs to Me, followed by a typical post‑drums block and then an encore‑style finish with U.S. Blues, Satisfaction and Brokedown Palace – creating a unique, memorable flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as being on top of a mellow, energetic vibe and “unleashing the beauty” throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for “bass‑bombs” that wowed the crowd, especially during Satisfaction.
band_member_comments.Drums: Highlighted as an intense, unpredictable part of the performance; drums/space segment noted as a surprise and a highlight.
